tasks.title: Install gdUnit4 Testing Framework
tasks.description: Install and configure the gdUnit4 addon for headless unit testing of pure GDScript modules
tasks.details:
1. Download gdUnit4 from Godot Asset Library or GitHub (https://github.com/MikeSchulze/gdUnit4)
2. Extract to `res://addons/gdUnit4/`
3. Enable the plugin in Project Settings → Plugins
4. Create `res://test/` directory for test files
5. Create a `TESTING.md` documenting the test approach
6. Verify installation by running gdUnit4 from the Godot editor

Pseudo-code for verification:
```gdscript
# res://test/sanity_test.gd
extends GdUnitTestSuite

func test_sanity() -> void:
    assert_bool(true).is_true()
```

tasks.title: Create Core Data Model Classes
tasks.description: Implement the fundamental RefCounted data classes that hold mutable game state: StatBlock, ResolvedStats, PlayerState, and GameState
tasks.details:
Create `res://logic/` directory and implement:

1. `stat_block.gd` - StatBlock (RefCounted):
```gdscript
class_name StatBlock extends RefCounted

var might: float = 1.0
var area: float = 1.0
var cooldown: float = 1.0
var amount: int = 0
var duration: float = 1.0
var speed: float = 1.0
var move_speed: float = 1.0
var max_health: float = 100.0
var recovery: float = 0.0
var armor: float = 0.0
var magnet: float = 64.0  # pixels
var luck: float = 1.0
var growth: float = 1.0
var greed: float = 1.0
var curse: float = 1.0
var revival: int = 0
```

2. `resolved_stats.gd` - ResolvedStats (computed each tick with caps):
```gdscript
class_name ResolvedStats extends RefCounted
# Same fields but computed values after multipliers and caps
```

3. `player_state.gd` - PlayerState:
```gdscript
class_name PlayerState extends RefCounted
var pos: Vector2 = Vector2.ZERO
var facing: Vector2 = Vector2.RIGHT
var velocity: Vector2 = Vector2.ZERO
var hp: float = 100.0
var level: int = 1
var xp: float = 0.0
var xp_to_next: float = 5.0
var iframe_timer: float = 0.0
var revivals: int = 0
var weapons: Array = []  # Array[WeaponInstance]
var passives: Array = []  # Array[PassiveInstance]
var stats: StatBlock = StatBlock.new()
var derived: ResolvedStats = ResolvedStats.new()
```

4. `game_state.gd` - GameState:
```gdscript
class_name GameState extends RefCounted
enum Phase { TITLE, PLAYING, PAUSED, LEVEL_UP, GAME_OVER, RESULTS }
var time_elapsed: float = 0.0
var current_minute: int = 0
var phase: int = Phase.TITLE
var rng: RandomNumberGenerator = RandomNumberGenerator.new()
var player: PlayerState = PlayerState.new()
var enemies: Array = []
var projectiles: Array = []
var zones: Array = []
var gems: Array = []
var pickups: Array = []
var chests: Array = []
var light_sources: Array = []
var index: SpatialIndex = null  # Set after SpatialIndex created
var spawn_cursor: int = 0
var event_cursor: int = 0
var chest_count: int = 0
var kills: int = 0
var gold: int = 0
var pending_levelups: int = 0
var current_offer = null
var global_effects: Dictionary = {}
```

Write unit tests for each class verifying default values and mutability.

tasks.title: Create Entity Data Classes
tasks.description: Implement data classes for game entities: Enemy, Projectile, DamageZone, Gem, Pickup, Chest, LightSource, WeaponInstance, PassiveInstance, LevelUpOffer
tasks.details:
Create in `res://logic/`:

1. `enemy.gd`:
```gdscript
class_name Enemy extends RefCounted
var def = null  # EnemyDef resource
var pos: Vector2 = Vector2.ZERO
var velocity: Vector2 = Vector2.ZERO
var hp: float = 1.0
var knockback: Vector2 = Vector2.ZERO
var knockback_timer: float = 0.0
var freeze_timer: float = 0.0
var is_boss: bool = false
var fixed_direction: bool = false
var floaty: bool = false
var hit_cooldowns: Dictionary = {}  # source_id -> timer
```

2. `projectile.gd`:
```gdscript
class_name Projectile extends RefCounted
var source_weapon = null
var pos: Vector2
var velocity: Vector2
var damage: float
var crit_mult: float = 1.0
var crit_chance: float = 0.0
var pierce_left: int = 1
var lifetime: float = 2.0
var bounces_left: int = 0
var hit_ids: PackedInt64Array = []
var is_boomerang: bool = false
var is_returning: bool = false
```

3. `damage_zone.gd`:
```gdscript
class_name DamageZone extends RefCounted
enum Anchor { FOLLOW_PLAYER, WORLD, ORBIT }
var source_weapon = null
var anchor: int = Anchor.WORLD
var pos: Vector2
var offset: Vector2 = Vector2.ZERO
var angle: float = 0.0
var radius: float = 32.0
var damage: float
var tick_interval: float = 0.5
var tick_timer: float = 0.0
var lifetime: float = 1.0
var hit_ids: PackedInt64Array = []
```

4. `gem.gd`, `pickup.gd`, `chest.gd`, `light_source.gd` with appropriate fields
5. `weapon_instance.gd` and `passive_instance.gd` for inventory items
6. `level_up_offer.gd` for the upgrade selection menu data

Write tests verifying construction and field access.

tasks.title: Create Immutable Data Definition Resources
tasks.description: Create the Resource-based definition classes for static game data: WeaponDef, EnemyDef, PassiveDef, StageDef, CharacterDef

tasks.title: Create Antonio Character and Whip Weapon Data
tasks.description: Author the concrete data files for Antonio (starting character) and the Whip (starting weapon) based on wiki specifications
tasks.details:
Create `.tres` resource files:

1. `res://data/character_antonio.tres`:
- Starting HP: 120 (Antonio gets +20% Max Health)
- Starting weapon: Whip
- Base stats: might 1.0, all others default
- Character bonus: +10% Might per level

2. `res://data/weapons/whip.tres`:
- Base damage: 20
- Cooldown: 1.3s
- Pierce: infinite within sweep
- Area: medium horizontal sweep
- Levels 1-8 progression (damage, area, amount scaling)
- Pattern: horizontal slash in facing direction, alternating sides

3. Create level curve data in `res://data/level_curve.gd`:
```gdscript
const CURVE: Array[int] = [5, 10, 20, 40, ...]  # XP needed for each level
const L20_BONUS_XP: int = 600
const L40_BONUS_XP: int = 2400
```

4. Create a basic test that loads and validates these resources.

tasks.title: Implement SpatialIndex System
tasks.description: Create the uniform spatial-hash grid system for efficient collision queries without physics nodes

tasks.title: Implement StatSystem
tasks.description: Create the pure stat resolution system that computes effective stats from base + passives + character + buffs with caps
tasks.details:
Create `res://logic/stat_system.gd`:

```gdscript
class_name StatSystem extends RefCounted

# Stat caps from the game
const MAX_COOLDOWN_REDUCTION: float = 0.9  # Can't go below 10% of base
const MAX_MOVE_SPEED_MULT: float = 2.0
const MAX_AREA_MULT: float = 3.0
const MIN_ARMOR: float = 0.0
const MAX_ARMOR: float = 100.0

static func resolve(player: PlayerState, stage_def = null) -> void:
    var derived = player.derived
    var block = player.stats
    
    # Start from base stats
    derived.might = block.might
    derived.area = block.area
    # ... etc
    
    # Add passive bonuses
    for passive in player.passives:
        _apply_passive(derived, passive)
    
    # Apply stage modifiers if any
    if stage_def:
        derived.curse *= stage_def.curse_mult
    
    # Apply caps
    derived.cooldown = maxf(1.0 - MAX_COOLDOWN_REDUCTION, derived.cooldown)
    derived.move_speed = minf(derived.move_speed, MAX_MOVE_SPEED_MULT)
    derived.area = minf(derived.area, MAX_AREA_MULT)
    derived.armor = clampf(derived.armor, MIN_ARMOR, MAX_ARMOR)

static func recompute_block(player: PlayerState) -> void:
    # Called when items change - rebuild block from inventory
    player.stats = StatBlock.new()  # Reset to defaults
    # Apply character base stats
    # Sum all passive contributions
```

Write tests verifying stat calculations, cap enforcement, and passive stacking.

tasks.title: Implement MovementSystem
tasks.description: Create the pure movement system for player and enemy position updates
tasks.details:
Create `res://logic/movement_system.gd`:

```gdscript
class_name MovementSystem extends RefCounted

const BASE_PLAYER_SPEED: float = 100.0  # pixels/sec

static func step_player(player: PlayerState, input_dir: Vector2, dt: float) -> void:
    # Normalize input and apply move speed
    if input_dir.length_squared() > 0.01:
        input_dir = input_dir.normalized()
        player.facing = input_dir
    
    var speed = BASE_PLAYER_SPEED * player.derived.move_speed
    player.velocity = input_dir * speed
    player.pos += player.velocity * dt

static func step_enemies(state: GameState, dt: float) -> void:
    var player_pos = state.player.pos
    
    for enemy in state.enemies:
        # Skip if frozen
        if enemy.freeze_timer > 0:
            enemy.freeze_timer -= dt
            continue
        
        # Apply knockback decay
        if enemy.knockback_timer > 0:
            enemy.knockback_timer -= dt
            enemy.pos += enemy.knockback * dt
            continue
        
        # Movement based on type
        if enemy.fixed_direction:
            # Swarm enemies move in fixed direction
            enemy.pos += enemy.velocity * dt
        else:
            # Home toward player
            var dir = (player_pos - enemy.pos).normalized()
            var speed = enemy.def.speed
            enemy.velocity = dir * speed
            enemy.pos += enemy.velocity * dt
        
        # Floaty sine offset for appropriate enemies
        if enemy.floaty:
            enemy.pos.y += sin(state.time_elapsed * 3.0) * 0.5
```

Write tests for player movement with various inputs, enemy homing, knockback decay, and freeze behavior.

tasks.title: Implement CombatMath Utilities
tasks.description: Create shared combat math functions for damage calculation, crits, and knockback
tasks.details:
Create `res://logic/combat_math.gd`:

```gdscript
class_name CombatMath extends RefCounted

const KNOCKBACK_DURATION: float = 0.1  # seconds
const BASE_KNOCKBACK_FORCE: float = 100.0

static func calc_damage(base_damage: float, might: float) -> float:
    return base_damage * might

static func roll_crit(rng: RandomNumberGenerator, crit_chance: float, crit_mult: float) -> Dictionary:
    var is_crit = rng.randf() < crit_chance
    return {
        "is_crit": is_crit,
        "multiplier": crit_mult if is_crit else 1.0
    }

static func apply_armor(damage: float, armor: float) -> float:
    # Damage is reduced by armor, minimum 1
    return maxf(damage - armor, 1.0)

static func calc_knockback(from_pos: Vector2, to_pos: Vector2, force: float, resist: float) -> Vector2:
    if resist >= 1.0:
        return Vector2.ZERO  # Boss immune
    var dir = (to_pos - from_pos).normalized()
    return dir * force * (1.0 - resist)

static func is_in_range(a: Vector2, b: Vector2, range_sq: float) -> bool:
    return a.distance_squared_to(b) <= range_sq
```

Write unit tests for each function with edge cases (zero armor, full resist, etc.).

tasks.title: Implement WeaponSystem Core
tasks.description: Create the weapon system that ticks cooldowns and emits projectiles/zones based on weapon patterns
tasks.details:
Create `res://logic/weapon_system.gd`:

```gdscript
class_name WeaponSystem extends RefCounted

static func step(state: GameState, dt: float) -> void:
    for weapon in state.player.weapons:
        weapon.cooldown_timer -= dt
        if weapon.cooldown_timer <= 0:
            cast(state, weapon)
            # Reset cooldown with player's cooldown reduction
            weapon.cooldown_timer = weapon.def.cooldown * state.player.derived.cooldown

static func cast(state: GameState, weapon: WeaponInstance) -> void:
    var def = weapon.def
    var player = state.player
    var amount = def.amount + player.derived.amount
    
    match def.id:
        "whip":
            _cast_whip(state, weapon, amount)
        "magic_wand":
            _cast_magic_wand(state, weapon, amount)
        "knife":
            _cast_knife(state, weapon, amount)
        # ... other weapons

static func _cast_whip(state: GameState, weapon: WeaponInstance, amount: int) -> void:
    # Horizontal slash in facing direction
    # Create a wide damage zone that hits all enemies in sweep
    var zone = DamageZone.new()
    zone.source_weapon = weapon
    zone.anchor = DamageZone.Anchor.FOLLOW_PLAYER
    zone.offset = state.player.facing * 40.0
    zone.radius = 60.0 * state.player.derived.area
    zone.damage = weapon.def.base_damage * _level_mult(weapon.level)
    zone.lifetime = 0.15  # Brief slash
    zone.tick_interval = 0.0  # Single hit
    state.zones.append(zone)
    
    # Alternate sides for next cast
    weapon.scratch["side"] = -weapon.scratch.get("side", 1)

static func _level_mult(level: int) -> float:
    # Damage scaling per level
    return 1.0 + (level - 1) * 0.1
```

Implement Whip pattern first, add other weapons incrementally. Write tests for cooldown ticking and projectile/zone creation.

tasks.title: Implement CombatSystem
tasks.description: Create the combat resolution system that processes projectile/zone hits against enemies
tasks.details:
Create `res://logic/combat_system.gd`:

```gdscript
class_name CombatSystem extends RefCounted

static func step(state: GameState, dt: float) -> void:
    _step_projectiles(state, dt)
    _step_zones(state, dt)

static func _step_projectiles(state: GameState, dt: float) -> void:
    var to_remove: Array[int] = []
    
    for i in state.projectiles.size():
        var proj = state.projectiles[i]
        proj.lifetime -= dt
        if proj.lifetime <= 0:
            to_remove.append(i)
            continue
        
        # Move projectile
        proj.pos += proj.velocity * dt
        
        # Check enemy hits via SpatialIndex
        var nearby = SpatialIndex.query_radius(state.index, proj.pos, 16.0)
        for enemy_idx in nearby:
            if enemy_idx in proj.hit_ids:
                continue  # Already hit this enemy
            
            var enemy = state.enemies[enemy_idx]
            _apply_hit(state, enemy, proj)
            proj.hit_ids.append(enemy_idx)
            proj.pierce_left -= 1
            
            if proj.pierce_left <= 0:
                to_remove.append(i)
                break
    
    # Remove expired/pierced projectiles (reverse order)
    for i in range(to_remove.size() - 1, -1, -1):
        _swap_remove(state.projectiles, to_remove[i])

static func _apply_hit(state: GameState, enemy: Enemy, proj: Projectile) -> void:
    var damage = CombatMath.calc_damage(proj.damage, state.player.derived.might)
    var crit = CombatMath.roll_crit(state.rng, proj.crit_chance, proj.crit_mult)
    damage *= crit.multiplier
    
    enemy.hp -= damage
    
    # Apply knockback
    var kb = CombatMath.calc_knockback(proj.pos, enemy.pos, 100.0, enemy.def.knockback_resist)
    if kb.length_squared() > 0:
        enemy.knockback = kb
        enemy.knockback_timer = 0.1
    
    if enemy.hp <= 0:
        _on_enemy_death(state, enemy)

static func _on_enemy_death(state: GameState, enemy: Enemy) -> void:
    state.kills += 1
    # Spawn gem
    var gem = Gem.new()
    gem.pos = enemy.pos
    gem.xp = enemy.def.xp_value
    state.gems.append(gem)
    # Roll for drops...
```

Write tests for projectile movement, hit detection, damage application, pierce mechanics, and enemy death spawns.

tasks.title: Implement SpawnDirector
tasks.description: Create the spawn system that manages enemy waves, bosses, events, and the 30-minute Reaper
tasks.details:
Create `res://logic/spawn_director.gd`:

```gdscript
class_name SpawnDirector extends RefCounted

const SPAWN_RING_MIN: float = 400.0  # Min distance from player
const SPAWN_RING_MAX: float = 500.0  # Max distance
const PERIODIC_HALT_CAP: int = 300
const HARD_CAP: int = 500
const REAPER_TIME: float = 1800.0  # 30 minutes

static func step(state: GameState, stage: StageDef, dt: float) -> void:
    state.time_elapsed += dt
    var new_minute = int(state.time_elapsed / 60.0)
    
    if new_minute > state.current_minute:
        state.current_minute = new_minute
        _on_minute_change(state, stage)
    
    # Check for Reaper spawn
    if state.time_elapsed >= REAPER_TIME and not _has_reaper(state):
        _spawn_reaper(state)
    
    # Top up enemies toward min-alive
    var wave = _get_current_wave(stage, state.current_minute)
    if wave and state.enemies.size() < PERIODIC_HALT_CAP:
        _spawn_wave_topup(state, wave)

static func _spawn_wave_topup(state: GameState, wave: Dictionary) -> void:
    var min_alive = wave.min_alive
    var curse = state.player.derived.curse
    var interval = wave.spawn_interval / curse
    
    while state.enemies.size() < min_alive and state.enemies.size() < HARD_CAP:
        var enemy = _create_enemy(state, wave.enemy_def)
        enemy.pos = _random_ring_pos(state.player.pos, SPAWN_RING_MIN, SPAWN_RING_MAX, state.rng)
        state.enemies.append(enemy)

static func _spawn_reaper(state: GameState) -> void:
    # Clear all non-immune enemies
    state.enemies.clear()
    # Spawn The Reaper
    var reaper = Enemy.new()
    reaper.def = GameData.get_enemy("reaper")
    reaper.hp = 65535
    reaper.pos = _random_ring_pos(state.player.pos, SPAWN_RING_MIN, SPAWN_RING_MAX, state.rng)
    reaper.is_boss = true
    state.enemies.append(reaper)

static func _random_ring_pos(center: Vector2, min_r: float, max_r: float, rng: RandomNumberGenerator) -> Vector2:
    var angle = rng.randf() * TAU
    var dist = rng.randf_range(min_r, max_r)
    return center + Vector2(cos(angle), sin(angle)) * dist
```

Write tests for wave spawning, cap enforcement, ring positioning, and Reaper trigger.

tasks.title: Implement PickupSystem
tasks.description: Create the system for magnetizing and collecting gems and pickups
tasks.details:
Create `res://logic/pickup_system.gd`:

```gdscript
class_name PickupSystem extends RefCounted

const COLLECTION_RADIUS: float = 16.0
const MAGNET_SPEED: float = 300.0
const GEM_CAP: int = 400

static func step(state: GameState, dt: float) -> void:
    var player_pos = state.player.pos
    var magnet_range = state.player.derived.magnet
    var growth = state.player.derived.growth
    var greed = state.player.derived.greed
    
    _step_gems(state, player_pos, magnet_range, growth, dt)
    _step_pickups(state, player_pos, dt)
    _step_chests(state, player_pos)
    _enforce_gem_cap(state)

static func _step_gems(state: GameState, player_pos: Vector2, magnet_range: float, growth: float, dt: float) -> void:
    var collected: Array[int] = []
    
    for i in state.gems.size():
        var gem = state.gems[i]
        var dist = player_pos.distance_to(gem.pos)
        
        if dist <= COLLECTION_RADIUS:
            # Collect gem
            var xp = gem.xp * growth
            ProgressionSystem.add_xp(state, xp)
            collected.append(i)
        elif dist <= magnet_range:
            # Magnetize toward player
            var dir = (player_pos - gem.pos).normalized()
            gem.pos += dir * MAGNET_SPEED * dt
    
    # Remove collected (reverse order)
    for i in range(collected.size() - 1, -1, -1):
        _swap_remove(state.gems, collected[i])

static func _enforce_gem_cap(state: GameState) -> void:
    if state.gems.size() > GEM_CAP:
        # Merge excess into one red gem
        var excess_xp = 0.0
        while state.gems.size() > GEM_CAP:
            excess_xp += state.gems.pop_back().xp
        if excess_xp > 0:
            var red_gem = Gem.new()
            red_gem.xp = excess_xp
            red_gem.tier = 2  # Red
            red_gem.pos = state.gems[GEM_CAP - 1].pos
            state.gems.append(red_gem)
```

Write tests for magnet behavior, collection, XP routing, and gem cap enforcement.

tasks.title: Implement ProgressionSystem
tasks.description: Create the XP/leveling system with level-up offer generation and item application
tasks.details:
Create `res://logic/progression_system.gd`:

```gdscript
class_name ProgressionSystem extends RefCounted

const MAX_WEAPONS: int = 6
const MAX_PASSIVES: int = 6

static func add_xp(state: GameState, amount: float) -> void:
    var player = state.player
    player.xp += amount
    
    while player.xp >= player.xp_to_next:
        player.xp -= player.xp_to_next
        player.level += 1
        state.pending_levelups += 1
        
        # Calculate next threshold from curve
        player.xp_to_next = GameData.get_xp_for_level(player.level + 1)
        
        # Special level bonuses
        if player.level == 20:
            add_xp(state, 600)  # L20 bonus
        elif player.level == 40:
            add_xp(state, 2400)  # L40 bonus

static func build_offer(state: GameState) -> LevelUpOffer:
    var offer = LevelUpOffer.new()
    var player = state.player
    var options: Array = []
    
    # Gather available options
    var available_weapons = _get_upgradeable_weapons(player)
    var available_passives = _get_upgradeable_passives(player)
    var new_weapons = _get_new_weapons(player) if player.weapons.size() < MAX_WEAPONS else []
    var new_passives = _get_new_passives(player) if player.passives.size() < MAX_PASSIVES else []
    
    # Build 3-4 options
    var pool = available_weapons + available_passives + new_weapons + new_passives
    pool.shuffle()  # Use state.rng for determinism
    
    var num_options = 3
    if state.rng.randf() < (1.0 - 1.0 / state.player.derived.luck):
        num_options = 4
    
    for i in mini(num_options, pool.size()):
        options.append(pool[i])
    
    offer.options = options
    return offer

static func apply_choice(state: GameState, index: int) -> void:
    var choice = state.current_offer.options[index]
    var player = state.player
    
    if choice.is_upgrade:
        choice.target.level += 1
    else:
        # New item
        if choice.kind == "weapon":
            var inst = WeaponInstance.new()
            inst.def = choice.def
            inst.level = 1
            player.weapons.append(inst)
        else:
            var inst = PassiveInstance.new()
            inst.def = choice.def
            inst.level = 1
            player.passives.append(inst)
    
    StatSystem.recompute_block(player)
    state.pending_levelups -= 1
```

Write tests for XP accumulation, level-up triggers, offer generation, choice application, and inventory limits.

tasks.title: Implement HealthSystem
tasks.description: Create the player health system with contact damage, i-frames, armor, recovery, and revival
tasks.details:
Create `res://logic/health_system.gd`:

```gdscript
class_name HealthSystem extends RefCounted

const IFRAME_DURATION: float = 0.24  # 240ms
const PLAYER_HITBOX: float = 16.0

static func step(state: GameState, dt: float) -> void:
    var player = state.player
    
    # Tick i-frame timer
    if player.iframe_timer > 0:
        player.iframe_timer -= dt
    
    # Apply recovery
    var recovery = player.derived.recovery
    if recovery > 0 and player.hp < player.derived.max_health:
        player.hp = minf(player.hp + recovery * dt, player.derived.max_health)
    
    # Check enemy contact damage
    if player.iframe_timer <= 0:
        _check_contact_damage(state)
    
    # Check death
    if player.hp <= 0:
        _on_death(state)

static func _check_contact_damage(state: GameState) -> void:
    var player = state.player
    var nearby = SpatialIndex.query_radius(state.index, player.pos, PLAYER_HITBOX)
    
    for enemy_idx in nearby:
        var enemy = state.enemies[enemy_idx]
        var raw_damage = enemy.def.power
        var damage = CombatMath.apply_armor(raw_damage, player.derived.armor)
        
        player.hp -= damage
        player.iframe_timer = IFRAME_DURATION
        
        # Only take damage from one enemy per contact
        break

static func _on_death(state: GameState) -> void:
    var player = state.player
    
    if player.revivals > 0:
        # Revive
        player.revivals -= 1
        player.hp = player.derived.max_health * 0.5
        player.iframe_timer = 1.0  # Burst i-frames on revive
    else:
        state.phase = GameState.Phase.GAME_OVER
```

Write tests for i-frame gating, armor application (min 1 damage), recovery tick, revival mechanics, and death transition.

tasks.title: Create GameData Autoload
tasks.description: Implement the autoload singleton that loads and exposes all immutable game data

tasks.title: Create RunController Orchestrator
tasks.description: Implement the main game controller that owns GameState, handles input, and calls systems in order each tick
tasks.details:
Create `res://game/run_controller.gd`:

```gdscript
extends Node2D
class_name RunController

signal level_up_started(offer: LevelUpOffer)
signal run_ended(summary: Dictionary)
signal phase_changed(phase: int)

var state: GameState = null
var _stage_def: StageDef = null

func _ready() -> void:
    _stage_def = GameData.get_stage("mad_forest")

func _physics_process(delta: float) -> void:
    if state == null or state.phase != GameState.Phase.PLAYING:
        return
    
    # 1. Input
    var input_dir = _get_input_direction()
    
    # 2. Stats
    StatSystem.resolve(state.player, _stage_def)
    
    # 3. Player movement
    MovementSystem.step_player(state.player, input_dir, delta)
    
    # 4. Spawning
    SpawnDirector.step(state, _stage_def, delta)
    
    # 5. Enemy movement
    MovementSystem.step_enemies(state, delta)
    
    # 6. Spatial index rebuild
    SpatialIndex.rebuild(state.index, state.enemies, state.gems, state.pickups)
    
    # 7. Weapons
    WeaponSystem.step(state, delta)
    
    # 8. Combat
    CombatSystem.step(state, delta)
    
    # 9. Pickups
    PickupSystem.step(state, delta)
    
    # 10. Health
    HealthSystem.step(state, delta)
    
    # 11. Phase check
    if state.pending_levelups > 0 and state.phase == GameState.Phase.PLAYING:
        state.current_offer = ProgressionSystem.build_offer(state)
        _set_phase(GameState.Phase.LEVEL_UP)
        level_up_started.emit(state.current_offer)

func _get_input_direction() -> Vector2:
    return Input.get_vector("move_left", "move_right", "move_up", "move_down")

func start_run(character_id: String = "antonio") -> void:
    state = GameState.new()
    state.rng.seed = Time.get_ticks_usec()
    state.index = SpatialIndex.new()
    
    # Initialize player from character def
    var char_def = GameData.get_character(character_id)
    state.player = _create_player_from_def(char_def)
    
    # 10 starting spawns
    for i in 10:
        SpawnDirector._spawn_wave_topup(state, _stage_def.waves[0])
    
    _set_phase(GameState.Phase.PLAYING)

func on_option_chosen(index: int) -> void:
    ProgressionSystem.apply_choice(state, index)
    state.current_offer = null
    
    if state.pending_levelups > 0:
        state.current_offer = ProgressionSystem.build_offer(state)
        level_up_started.emit(state.current_offer)
    else:
        state.player.iframe_timer = 0.5  # Brief post-levelup i-frames
        _set_phase(GameState.Phase.PLAYING)

func _set_phase(phase: int) -> void:
    state.phase = phase
    phase_changed.emit(phase)
```

Create `res://game/Main.tscn` with RunController as root.

tasks.title: Create Input Actions
tasks.description: Configure the input map for player movement and UI interactions

tasks.title: Create PresentationLayer and EntityRenderer
tasks.description: Implement the visual rendering system with pooled sprites for all entity types
tasks.details:
Create `res://game/presentation_layer.gd`:

```gdscript
extends Node2D
class_name PresentationLayer

# Sprite pools per entity category
var _enemy_pool: Array[Sprite2D] = []
var _projectile_pool: Array[Sprite2D] = []
var _zone_pool: Array[Sprite2D] = []
var _gem_pool: Array[Sprite2D] = []
var _pickup_pool: Array[Sprite2D] = []
var _player_sprite: Sprite2D = null

const POOL_INITIAL_SIZE: int = 100

func _ready() -> void:
    _init_pools()
    _create_player_sprite()

func _init_pools() -> void:
    for i in POOL_INITIAL_SIZE:
        _enemy_pool.append(_create_sprite())
        _projectile_pool.append(_create_sprite())
        _gem_pool.append(_create_sprite())

func _create_sprite() -> Sprite2D:
    var sprite = Sprite2D.new()
    sprite.visible = false
    add_child(sprite)
    return sprite

func sync(state: GameState) -> void:
    _sync_player(state.player)
    _sync_entities(state.enemies, _enemy_pool, "enemy")
    _sync_entities(state.projectiles, _projectile_pool, "projectile")
    _sync_entities(state.zones, _zone_pool, "zone")
    _sync_entities(state.gems, _gem_pool, "gem")
    _sync_entities(state.pickups, _pickup_pool, "pickup")

func _sync_player(player: PlayerState) -> void:
    _player_sprite.position = player.pos
    # Flip based on facing
    _player_sprite.flip_h = player.facing.x < 0

func _sync_entities(entities: Array, pool: Array[Sprite2D], type: String) -> void:
    # Hide all sprites first
    for sprite in pool:
        sprite.visible = false
    
    # Expand pool if needed
    while pool.size() < entities.size():
        pool.append(_create_sprite())
    
    # Position visible sprites
    for i in entities.size():
        var sprite = pool[i]
        sprite.position = entities[i].pos
        sprite.visible = true
        # Set texture/modulate based on entity type and def
        _apply_visual(sprite, entities[i], type)

func _apply_visual(sprite: Sprite2D, entity, type: String) -> void:
    match type:
        "enemy":
            sprite.texture = entity.def.texture if entity.def else preload("res://icon.svg")
        "gem":
            sprite.modulate = [Color.CYAN, Color.GREEN, Color.RED][entity.tier]
        # etc.
```

Integrate with RunController's render step. Use placeholder textures initially.

tasks.title: Create HUD UI
tasks.description: Implement the heads-up display showing XP bar, timer, level, gold, kills, and HP
tasks.details:
Create `res://ui/hud.tscn` and `res://ui/hud.gd`:

```gdscript
extends Control
class_name HUD

@onready var xp_bar: ProgressBar = $XPBar
@onready var hp_bar: ProgressBar = $HPBar
@onready var timer_label: Label = $TimerLabel
@onready var level_label: Label = $LevelLabel
@onready var gold_label: Label = $GoldLabel
@onready var kills_label: Label = $KillsLabel

func update_from_state(state: GameState) -> void:
    var player = state.player
    
    # XP bar
    xp_bar.max_value = player.xp_to_next
    xp_bar.value = player.xp
    
    # HP bar
    hp_bar.max_value = player.derived.max_health
    hp_bar.value = player.hp
    
    # Timer (MM:SS format)
    var minutes = int(state.time_elapsed / 60)
    var seconds = int(state.time_elapsed) % 60
    timer_label.text = "%02d:%02d" % [minutes, seconds]
    
    # Stats
    level_label.text = "Lv %d" % player.level
    gold_label.text = str(state.gold)
    kills_label.text = str(state.kills)
```

Scene structure:
- HUD (Control, full_rect anchors)
  - XPBar (ProgressBar, top)
  - HPBar (ProgressBar, below XP)
  - TimerLabel (Label, top-right)
  - LevelLabel (Label)
  - GoldLabel (Label)
  - KillsLabel (Label)

Connect to RunController to update each frame.

tasks.title: Create Level-Up Screen UI
tasks.description: Implement the modal overlay for selecting level-up options
tasks.details:
Create `res://ui/level_up_screen.tscn` and `res://ui/level_up_screen.gd`:

```gdscript
extends Control
class_name LevelUpScreen

signal option_chosen(index: int)

@onready var options_container: VBoxContainer = $Panel/VBoxContainer
var _option_buttons: Array[Button] = []

func _ready() -> void:
    hide()

func show_offer(offer: LevelUpOffer) -> void:
    # Clear existing buttons
    for btn in _option_buttons:
        btn.queue_free()
    _option_buttons.clear()
    
    # Create button for each option
    for i in offer.options.size():
        var opt = offer.options[i]
        var btn = Button.new()
        btn.text = _format_option(opt)
        btn.pressed.connect(_on_option_pressed.bind(i))
        options_container.add_child(btn)
        _option_buttons.append(btn)
    
    show()
    _option_buttons[0].grab_focus()

func _format_option(opt: Dictionary) -> String:
    if opt.is_upgrade:
        return "%s Lv %d → %d" % [opt.def.name, opt.target_level - 1, opt.target_level]
    else:
        return "NEW: %s" % opt.def.name

func _on_option_pressed(index: int) -> void:
    hide()
    option_chosen.emit(index)
```

Scene structure:
- LevelUpScreen (Control, full_rect, semi-transparent bg)
  - Panel (PanelContainer, centered)
    - VBoxContainer
      - TitleLabel "LEVEL UP!"
      - (Buttons added dynamically)

Connect signal to RunController.on_option_chosen().

tasks.title: Create AudioService Stub
tasks.description: Implement a minimal audio service autoload for placeholder sound effects
tasks.details:
Create `res://autoload/audio_service.gd`:

```gdscript
extends Node

const POOL_SIZE: int = 8
var _sfx_pool: Array[AudioStreamPlayer] = []
var _pool_index: int = 0

# Placeholder - actual sounds loaded when available
var _sounds: Dictionary = {
    "hit": null,
    "death": null,
    "level_up": null,
    "pickup": null,
    "heal": null,
    "chest": null,
    "hurt": null,
    "weapon_fire": null
}

func _ready() -> void:
    for i in POOL_SIZE:
        var player = AudioStreamPlayer.new()
        add_child(player)
        _sfx_pool.append(player)

func play(sound_name: String) -> void:
    var sound = _sounds.get(sound_name)
    if sound == null:
        return  # No sound loaded yet
    
    var player = _sfx_pool[_pool_index]
    player.stream = sound
    player.play()
    _pool_index = (_pool_index + 1) % POOL_SIZE

func set_sound(name: String, stream: AudioStream) -> void:
    _sounds[name] = stream
```

Register as autoload in project.godot. Called from systems when events occur.

tasks.title: Add Additional Weapons
tasks.description: Implement weapon patterns for Magic Wand, Knife, Axe, Cross, King Bible, Fire Wand, Garlic, Santa Water
tasks.details:
Extend WeaponSystem with patterns for each weapon:

1. Magic Wand - fires at nearest enemy:
```gdscript
static func _cast_magic_wand(state: GameState, weapon: WeaponInstance, amount: int) -> void:
    var target_idx = SpatialIndex.nearest_enemy(state.index, state.player.pos)
    if target_idx < 0:
        return
    var target = state.enemies[target_idx]
    for i in amount:
        var proj = Projectile.new()
        proj.pos = state.player.pos
        proj.velocity = (target.pos - state.player.pos).normalized() * 300.0
        proj.damage = weapon.def.base_damage * _level_mult(weapon.level)
        state.projectiles.append(proj)
```

2. Knife - fires in facing direction, high speed
3. Axe - lobs upward with gravity arc, high damage
4. Cross - boomerang pattern, returns to player
5. King Bible - orbiting zones around player
6. Fire Wand - random target, creates brief explosion zone
7. Garlic - persistent aura zone around player with tick damage
8. Santa Water - drops puddles that persist and tick

Create .tres files for each in `res://data/weapons/` with appropriate base stats and level scaling.

tasks.title: Implement Chest System
tasks.description: Complete the chest drop and opening mechanics with beginner's luck and item selection
tasks.details:
Enhance ProgressionSystem and CombatSystem for chest handling:

```gdscript
# In progression_system.gd
const BEGINNER_LUCK_SEQUENCE: Array[int] = [1, 1, 3, 1, 1, 5]

static func open_chest(state: GameState, chest: Chest) -> Array:
    var results: Array = []
    var count = chest.rolled_count
    
    # Roll items
    for i in count:
        var item = _roll_chest_item(state)
        if item:
            results.append(item)
            _apply_item_to_player(state, item)
        else:
            # All maxed - give gold instead
            results.append({"type": "gold", "amount": 25})
            state.gold += 25
    
    return results

static func _determine_chest_count(state: GameState) -> int:
    if state.chest_count < BEGINNER_LUCK_SEQUENCE.size():
        return BEGINNER_LUCK_SEQUENCE[state.chest_count]
    # After beginner's luck, scale with player luck
    var roll = state.rng.randf()
    var luck = state.player.derived.luck
    if roll < 0.1 * luck:
        return 5
    elif roll < 0.3 * luck:
        return 3
    return 1
```

Boss death in CombatSystem spawns a chest with pre-rolled count.

tasks.title: Implement Light Source and Brazier System
tasks.description: Add breakable light sources that drop items from a weighted pool
tasks.details:
Create LightSource entity handling:

```gdscript
# In light_source.gd
class_name LightSource extends RefCounted
var pos: Vector2
var hp: float = 10.0

# In combat_system.gd - add light source damage
static func _damage_light_sources(state: GameState) -> void:
    var to_remove: Array[int] = []
    
    for i in state.light_sources.size():
        var light = state.light_sources[i]
        # Check if hit by any active zone/projectile
        for zone in state.zones:
            if light.pos.distance_to(zone.pos) < zone.radius:
                light.hp -= zone.damage
                break
        
        if light.hp <= 0:
            _on_light_break(state, light)
            to_remove.append(i)
    
    for i in range(to_remove.size() - 1, -1, -1):
        _swap_remove(state.light_sources, to_remove[i])

static func _on_light_break(state: GameState, light: LightSource) -> void:
    # Roll drop from weighted table
    var roll = state.rng.randf()
    var drop_type = PickupTable.roll_brazier_drop(roll)
    if drop_type:
        var pickup = Pickup.new()
        pickup.pos = light.pos
        pickup.type = drop_type
        state.pickups.append(pickup)
```

SpawnDirector spawns braziers periodically around the map edge.

tasks.title: Implement Special Pickups
tasks.description: Add vacuum (collect all gems), rosary (kill all enemies), orologion (freeze), and other special items
tasks.details:
Extend PickupSystem to handle special pickup types:

```gdscript
static func _apply_pickup(state: GameState, pickup: Pickup) -> void:
    match pickup.type:
        "chicken":
            state.player.hp = minf(state.player.hp + 30, state.player.derived.max_health)
        "coin_bag":
            state.gold += pickup.value
        "vacuum":
            _vacuum_all_gems(state)
        "rosary":
            _kill_all_enemies(state)
        "orologion":
            _freeze_all_enemies(state, 8.0)  # 8 second freeze
        "nduja":
            _apply_temp_buff(state, "might", 2.0, 10.0)  # 2x might for 10s
        "clover":
            _apply_temp_buff(state, "luck", 2.0, 10.0)

static func _vacuum_all_gems(state: GameState) -> void:
    for gem in state.gems:
        gem.pos = state.player.pos  # Instant collect next tick

static func _kill_all_enemies(state: GameState) -> void:
    for enemy in state.enemies:
        if not enemy.is_boss:
            _on_enemy_death(state, enemy)  # Spawn gems
    state.enemies = state.enemies.filter(func(e): return e.is_boss)

static func _freeze_all_enemies(state: GameState, duration: float) -> void:
    for enemy in state.enemies:
        enemy.freeze_timer = duration
```

tasks.title: Wire Complete Game Loop
tasks.description: Connect all systems, UI, and state transitions into a playable vertical slice

tasks.title: Add Integration Tests for Core Game Loop
tasks.description: Write gdUnit4 tests that exercise the full simulation pipeline
tasks.details:
Create `res://test/integration/` with comprehensive tests:

```gdscript
# game_loop_test.gd
extends GdUnitTestSuite

func test_full_tick_pipeline() -> void:
    var state = _create_test_state()
    state.phase = GameState.Phase.PLAYING
    
    # Simulate 60 ticks (1 second)
    for i in 60:
        _run_tick(state, 1.0/60.0, Vector2.RIGHT)
    
    # Player should have moved
    assert_float(state.player.pos.x).is_greater(0)
    # Time should have advanced
    assert_float(state.time_elapsed).is_equal_approx(1.0, 0.01)

func test_enemy_spawns_and_dies() -> void:
    var state = _create_test_state()
    state.phase = GameState.Phase.PLAYING
    
    # Give player a weapon
    _add_weapon(state.player, "whip")
    
    # Spawn an enemy near player
    var enemy = _spawn_enemy_at(state, state.player.pos + Vector2(50, 0))
    
    # Run ticks until enemy dies or timeout
    var ticks = 0
    while state.enemies.size() > 0 and ticks < 300:
        _run_tick(state, 1.0/60.0, Vector2.ZERO)
        ticks += 1
    
    assert_int(state.enemies.size()).is_equal(0)
    assert_int(state.kills).is_equal(1)
    assert_int(state.gems.size()).is_greater(0)

func test_level_up_flow() -> void:
    var state = _create_test_state()
    state.phase = GameState.Phase.PLAYING
    
    # Add enough XP to level up
    ProgressionSystem.add_xp(state, 100)
    
    assert_int(state.pending_levelups).is_greater(0)
```

Test determinism by running same seed twice and comparing results.

tasks.title: Performance Profiling and Optimization
tasks.description: Profile the game at scale (300-500 enemies) and optimize bottlenecks
tasks.details:
1. Create a stress test scenario:
```gdscript
# Debug command to spawn max enemies
func _debug_spawn_stress_test() -> void:
    while state.enemies.size() < 500:
        SpawnDirector._spawn_enemy_random(state)
```

2. Profile with Godot's built-in profiler:
- Check `_physics_process` time
- Monitor node count
- Track memory usage

3. Likely optimization targets:
- SpatialIndex.rebuild() - ensure O(n) not O(n²)
- Array operations - use swap-remove not erase
- Presentation sync - consider MultiMeshInstance2D if sprite count is issue

4. Target metrics:
- 500 enemies + 200 gems + 50 projectiles
- Stable 60 FPS
- Physics tick < 8ms

5. If needed, implement MultiMesh escalation:
```gdscript
# Use MultiMeshInstance2D for large entity counts
var enemy_multimesh: MultiMeshInstance2D

func _sync_enemies_multimesh(enemies: Array) -> void:
    enemy_multimesh.multimesh.instance_count = enemies.size()
    for i in enemies.size():
        var xform = Transform2D(0, enemies[i].pos)
        enemy_multimesh.multimesh.set_instance_transform_2d(i, xform)
```

tasks.title: Add Golden Path Test
tasks.description: Create a deterministic replay test that validates a known-good run sequence
tasks.details:
Create a golden path test that replays a fixed input sequence with a fixed seed and validates the final state:

```gdscript
# golden_path_test.gd
extends GdUnitTestSuite

const GOLDEN_SEED: int = 12345
const GOLDEN_INPUT_SEQUENCE: Array = [
    # [frame, input_vector]
    [0, Vector2.RIGHT],
    [60, Vector2.DOWN],
    [120, Vector2.LEFT],
    # ... recorded input for a full test run
]

func test_golden_path_determinism() -> void:
    var state1 = _run_golden_path(GOLDEN_SEED)
    var state2 = _run_golden_path(GOLDEN_SEED)
    
    # Same seed + same inputs = same result
    assert_float(state1.time_elapsed).is_equal(state2.time_elapsed)
    assert_int(state1.kills).is_equal(state2.kills)
    assert_int(state1.player.level).is_equal(state2.player.level)
    assert_vector2(state1.player.pos).is_equal(state2.player.pos)

func test_golden_path_expected_outcome() -> void:
    var state = _run_golden_path(GOLDEN_SEED)
    
    # Expected outcomes for this specific seed/input
    assert_int(state.kills).is_equal(EXPECTED_KILLS)
    assert_int(state.player.level).is_equal(EXPECTED_LEVEL)

func _run_golden_path(seed: int) -> GameState:
    var state = GameState.new()
    state.rng.seed = seed
    # ... initialize from Antonio/Mad Forest
    
    var frame = 0
    var input_idx = 0
    var current_input = Vector2.ZERO
    
    while frame < GOLDEN_RUN_FRAMES:
        if input_idx < GOLDEN_INPUT_SEQUENCE.size():
            if GOLDEN_INPUT_SEQUENCE[input_idx][0] == frame:
                current_input = GOLDEN_INPUT_SEQUENCE[input_idx][1]
                input_idx += 1
        
        _run_tick(state, 1.0/60.0, current_input)
        frame += 1
    
    return state
```

This catches regressions when systems are modified.
